question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with no limit and a startTime that matches a topic with millions of messages, causing findByFilter() to load maxPageSize=5000 rows per poll, and with the Flux.fromStream() not applying backpressure to the JPA stream, causing all 5000 objects to be materialized simultaneously and exhausting heap on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with the minimum allowed interval (50ms) and no limit, causing the withFixedDelay(interval) in RepeatSpec to fire poll() at maximum frequency, and with many concurrent subscribers generating a sustained high-frequency DB query storm that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that poll() calls filter.toBuilder() on every invocation to create new TopicMessageFilter objects with new random subscriberId values (from RandomStringUtils.random), causing excessive object churn and GC pressure that degrades 30%+ of nodes over time?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum subscriber count check in listen() to open subscriptions up to the gRPC server's connection limit, with each subscription holding a PollingContext and scheduler resources, collectively exhausting JVM heap and crashing 30%+ of mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with startTime set to 0 (nanoseconds epoch) and no endTime, causing the JPA criteria query in findByFilter() to perform a full index scan from the beginning of time on every poll cycle, generating excessive DB I/O that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) combined with withScheduler(scheduler) to pin Long.MAX_VALUE repeat-state objects in the scheduler's task queue, causing memory exhaustion in the boundedElastic scheduler's internal queue on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where messages are being written at a rate equal to maxPageSize per interval, causing poll() to always return a full page and never advance the startTime window fast enough, creating a perpetual catch-up loop that holds DB connections open indefinitely and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .name(METRIC).tag(METRIC_TAG, 'poll') metric registration in listen() to create a metric time series per subscription that accumulates in the MeterRegistry, causing unbounded metric memory growth that crashes 30%+ of nodes when thousands of subscriptions are opened?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that poll() uses Math.min(limit, listenerProperties.getMaxPageSize()) where maxPageSize defaults to 5000, and by opening many subscriptions each fetching 5000 rows per poll, generate enough combined DB result set data to exhaust the DB server's memory and crash 30%+ of mirror nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions that are never cancelled (persistent gRPC streams), causing the Flux pipeline with RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) to run indefinitely, and with each subscription holding a PollingContext, scheduler tasks, and DB connections, collectively exhausting all resources on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of query timeout in the findByFilter() call within poll() to submit subscriptions targeting a topic with a complex query plan, causing long-running DB queries that hold DB connections open and starve other operations, degrading 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(() -> poll(context)) pattern to cause deferred execution to pile up on the boundedElastic scheduler when the scheduler is saturated, creating a growing backlog of deferred tasks that eventually causes the scheduler to reject new tasks and crash 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: PollingContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where onNext() is called with a TopicMessage whose consensusTimestamp is Long.MAX_VALUE, causing last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 in poll() to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE, resulting in a query that scans the entire table and crashes 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a limit value of Long.MAX_VALUE, causing (int)(Long.MAX_VALUE - 0) to overflow to -1 in the integer cast, resulting in a negative pageSize passed to Math.min() that produces an unexpected value and corrupts the query behavior, potentially causing repeated errors that destabilize 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the withScheduler(scheduler) in RepeatSpec to cause the shared boundedElastic scheduler to accumulate repeat-delay tasks for Long.MAX_VALUE iterations, causing the scheduler's internal priority queue to grow unboundedly and exhaust heap memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic with a very large number of messages and no limit, causing each poll() to return a full page of 5000 messages, and with doOnNext(context::onNext) being called 5000 times per cycle updating AtomicLong and AtomicReference, generating excessive CPU contention on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of backpressure handling in PollingTopicListener.listen() (unlike SharedTopicListener which has onBackpressureBuffer) to cause unbounded message accumulation in the reactive pipeline when the downstream gRPC client is slow, exhausting heap memory on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with startTime set to a timestamp just before a known high-volume burst of messages, causing poll() to return maxPageSize=5000 messages on every cycle and never catching up, holding the subscription open indefinitely and consuming DB connections and heap on 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open subscriptions with unique topicId values for each connection, causing the DB query planner to cache a separate query plan per topicId, exhausting the DB's plan cache memory and degrading query performance across 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByFilter() uses getResultList().stream() (loading all results into memory before streaming) to cause each poll() call to allocate a full List<TopicMessage> of up to 5000 objects in heap, and with many concurrent subscriptions, exhaust heap memory and crash 30%+ of nodes?
